untranslatable_reason: Signal requires subjective 'pattern identification' (support/resistance, breakout structure) which cannot be expressed as discrete indicator thresholds. The core rule is qualitative (confirmation bias prevention) rather than a quantifiable pattern. Translated using pivot points and RSI as proxy confirmations, but true pattern detection requires candlestick/structure analysis outside allowed indicators.
translation_notes: Used pivot/support/resistance (s1, r1) as proxy for pattern levels; RSI overbought/oversold (>70/<30) for independent indicator confirmation; volume ratio for volume confirmation; context_dependent direction reflects that signal applies to both long and short patterns.
